text.title: Type Annotation Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Any overuse, bare type: ignore, cast() misuse, @runtime_checkable on hot paths, and mixed union styles.

antipattern.description: Using `Any` as a shortcut to silence mypy errors on unknown types instead of properly annotating the shape.
antipattern.reason: Every `Any` annotation is a gap in type coverage. mypy treats `Any` as compatible with every type, meaning errors flow through silently. A function returning `Any` propagates the gap to all callers — one `Any` can suppress errors across an entire call chain.

antipattern.description: Importing `Any` at module level and using it for dict payloads instead of defining `TypedDict` or Pydantic models.
antipattern.reason: Dict-typed returns (`dict[str, Any]`) are the most common `Any` sink. Replacing them with `TypedDict` definitions costs little and gives mypy visibility into key access patterns.

antipattern.description: Adding `# type: ignore` without a specific error code, e.g., `# type: ignore` instead of `# type: ignore[attr-defined]`.
antipattern.reason: Bare `# type: ignore` silences all mypy errors on the line, including future ones unrelated to the original issue. A scoped code silences only the specific error class. `warn_unused_ignores = true` in mypy config flags stale ignores when the underlying issue is fixed.

example.note: Bare ignore — silences future unrelated errors
example.code: result = some_dynamic_call() # type: ignore # Bad: too broad
result = some_dynamic_call() # type: ignore[no-any-return] # dynamic ORM result

antipattern.description: Using `cast(SomeType, value)` to satisfy mypy instead of adding a proper narrowing guard (`isinstance`, `assert`, or TypeIs function).
antipattern.reason: `cast()` is a lie to the type checker — it makes no runtime assertion. If the actual type differs, the bug propagates silently. A narrowing guard (`if isinstance(x, SomeType): ...`) is both a type assertion and a runtime safety check.

antipattern.description: Mixing `Optional[X]` and `X | None` in the same codebase, or mixing `Union[X, Y]` and `X | Y`.
antipattern.reason: Mixed styles produce inconsistent grep results and confuse `ruff --fix` idempotency. Pick `X | None` / `X | Y` for all new code and enforce with ruff UP007. Existing `Optional` imports can be batch-migrated with `ruff check --select UP --fix`.

antipattern.description: Applying `@runtime_checkable` to a Protocol that is checked with `isinstance()` in a tight loop or frequently-called middleware.
antipattern.reason: `isinstance(obj, SomeProtocol)` with `@runtime_checkable` checks only method presence, not signatures, and does so on every call. The overhead is measurable on hot paths. Use it only when the check is infrequent and the dynamic dispatch is genuinely needed.
